status_messages.what_was_done: Collected intent: target='customers', purpose='based on customer's shopping behavior and preferences to clu'
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that the dataset file actually exists.

user_intent.business_purpose: based on customer's shopping behavior and preferences to cluster them and label each cluster

status_messages.what_was_done: Profiled 50,000×23 dataset. Found 11 numeric cols. Claude suggested 7 feature groups.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not sample sub-populations or run hypothesis tests for distribution differences.
status_messages.doubts: Suggested groups are based on column names; actual feature buildability depends on data quality. Mean skewness=3.6.
issues: Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

spending_behavior.description: Captures how much a customer spends in aggregate, on average, and the variability of their spending habits
spending_behavior.rationale: Spend level and consistency are primary differentiators of customer segments (e.g., high-value vs. budget shoppers), directly driving marketing and loyalty strategy

transaction_frequency_recency.description: Captures how often a customer transacts and how recently they were active, forming an RFM-style behavioral signal
transaction_frequency_recency.rationale: Frequency and recency are core RFM dimensions that distinguish loyal frequent buyers from occasional or lapsed customers, critical for retention-focused clustering

category_preferences.description: Captures which merchant categories a customer favors, their category diversity, and dominant spend category
category_preferences.rationale: Category affinity reveals lifestyle and interest segments (e.g., health-focused, entertainment-driven) enabling personalized product recommendations and targeted campaigns

geographic_mobility.description: Captures how far a customer typically travels from their home location to transact, and whether they shop locally or widely
geographic_mobility.rationale: Geographic spread of transactions reveals online vs. local shoppers and frequent travelers, which informs channel strategy and regional offer relevance

temporal_patterns.description: Captures preferred time-of-day, day-of-week, and seasonal shopping patterns derived from transaction timestamps
temporal_patterns.rationale: Shopping timing patterns distinguish morning routine buyers from late-night impulse shoppers and weekend vs. weekday shoppers, enabling time-targeted engagement

demographic_profile.description: Captures customer age (derived from date of birth) and gender as stable demographic anchors to enrich behavioral clusters
demographic_profile.rationale: Demographics provide interpretable labeling context for clusters and help distinguish life-stage segments (e.g., young urban professionals vs. retired suburban shoppers)

fraud_risk_profile.description: Captures the proportion of a customer's transactions flagged as fraudulent and their overall fraud exposure risk level
fraud_risk_profile.rationale: Fraud rate per customer is a critical behavioral signal that can isolate a high-risk segment for dedicated monitoring and intervention, protecting business and customer trust

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 108 features (PCA+AE). Removed 76 via VIF gate. Claude selected 30 from 32 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).

status_messages.what_was_done: Clustered with k=6, ran deepening loop
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Could not resolve oversized cluster C0
status_messages.doubts: Oversized cluster may reflect feature redundancy

claude_decision.reasoning: The current features (gas_transport, home, grocery_pos) appear too broad and overlapping to meaningfully separate this dominant cluster, as these are common spending categories shared by a majority of customers. Returning to feature selection to identify more discriminative features that can better differentiate spending behaviors within this large group.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 108 features (PCA+AE). Removed 76 via VIF gate. Claude selected 29 from 32 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).

status_messages.what_was_done: Used AgglomerativeClustering with k=6 (auto-selected via silhouette). Ran deepening loop → 8 leaf clusters. Silhouette=0.1669 (weak).
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not try DBSCAN or GMM (not in current skill set).
issues: Silhouette=0.167 < 0.25 — clusters may overlap. Consider different k or algorithm.

status_messages.what_was_done: Named 8 clusters using Claude (tone='easy'). Clarity Gate PASSED. Avg confidence=7.5/10.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that description text references specific numbers (only checked confidence and name uniqueness).

persona_names.1: The Light-Touch Spender Across the Board
persona_names.2: The Rare But Big-Ticket Online Shopper
persona_names.3: The High-Frequency Churner Across All Categories
persona_names.4: The Loyal Heavy Online-and-In-Store Power Shopper
persona_names.6: The Steady Gas & Everyday Errand Runner (Low Online Grocery)
persona_names.7: The High-Frequency Multi-Category Daily Transactor (Heaviest Volume)
persona_names.8: The Moderate-Volume Traveller With High Spend Per Trip

status_messages.what_was_done: Trained RF, 5-fold stratified CV. CV macro-F1=0.948 ≥ 0.7 threshold.
status_messages.doubts: Lowest-F1 personas: The Moderate-Volume Traveller With High Spend Per Trip(0.82), The Steady Gas & Everyday Errand Runner (Low Online Grocery)(0.89), The High-Frequency Multi-Category Daily Transactor (Heaviest Volume)(0.89)

route oversized cluster C0 (55%): sub-cluster or reselect features
